recommendation_chain.option: (i) Push EXP-295 P0 to priority queue for integration into data/sections.json (RECOMMENDED)
rationale: Operator's explicit Option D choice in EXP-295 has never been countermanded; this completes the originally-scoped work.
Curmudgeon-on-EXP-295 (consolidation 2026-05-09T18:40Z) endorsed Option D and identified one concrete prose error which has since been fixed (HNOTE 2026-05-10).

The anchor in P0 ('closing </details> of p6-612-11') is still exactly correct: byte-exact gap between p6-612's closing tag and p6-614's opening tag is a single '\n' (no intervening content, no other subsection inserted in between).

Without Part 6.13, five 'Self-Contradicted' verdicts cite an implicit 'MOND grouping' frame in their sub_rationales (audit-trail layer) that has no single-place explanation in the public site — readers see the verdicts but not the structural argument they rest on.
p6-614 (κ-cluster) was integrated via EXP-301, so the numbering reads 6.12 → (gap) → 6.14 today — fixing the gap is structurally cleaner than leaving it permanent.

The c4 review's advocate_mode flagged a 'dome silent on extra-firmamental geometry' defense (defense_survives=3); a one-sentence preemption can be appended to the proposed Part 6.13 prose during integration (see proposed_addendum below).
recommendation_chain.operator_action_needed: None for the integration itself; decider can push EXP-295 P0 to priority queue with target_type='section-new', class='deep-attack' (the c4 advocate-mode-3 defense warrants curmudgeon adversarial pass before publish). The proposed_subsection_html in EXP-295 is the integration source; this EXP-424 file is the routing decision, not a replacement of that HTML.

recommendation_chain.option: (ii) Close ISS-2103 as already-resolved-by-supersession; do NOT integrate Part 6.13
rationale_for: ISS-2103's premise (dangling sub_rationale cross-references to Part 6.13) is stale — those references were removed 2 days before ISS-2103 was created.
Nine days of non-integration (2026-05-10 → 2026-05-18) with no operator nudge could be interpreted as implicit deprioritization.
Part 6 is already 122 KB; adding 13.8 KB lengthens an already long section.

The cross-cutting argument is partially redundant with per-WIN detail_evidence prose (WIN-048 already says 'Self-contradiction with WIN-034' inline; WIN-054 already presents the El Gordo dilemma inline).
Integrating now would re-open curmudgeon review surface (c4 advocate-mode defense_survives=3 means non-trivial adversarial work) for a section the operator did not push back on.
why_this_option_is_NOT_recommended: It treats operator silence as implicit veto, which is not how the rest of the pipeline reads operator notes. Until the operator explicitly retracts Option D, Option D is the standing instruction.

It accepts the audit-trail-only convention permanently (so the 'MOND grouping' tag on five verdicts never gets a public explainer), which is a quiet rendering-debt that the c4 hole #2 also flagged as worth fixing separately.

The integration friction is small (one P0 patch, one curmudgeon adversarial review, one publish) compared to the analytical work already invested (~13.8 KB of operator+analyst+curmudgeon-reviewed prose with wording-fix audit trail).
The gap in numbering (6.12 → 6.14) is itself a visible defect to careful readers.

proposed_integration_patches.action: Push EXP-295 P0 (Part 6.13 MOND grouping subsection) to the curmudgeon priority queue for adversarial review and integration. target_type='section-new'; review_class='deep-attack' (c4 advocate-mode defense_survives=3 means a fresh adversarial pass before publish is warranted). The integration source is monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json:patches[0] plus proposed_subsection_html (with wording_fix_2026_05_10 already applied).
proposed_integration_patches.notes: This EXP-424 file is the routing decision. The actual subsection HTML is in EXP-295, not duplicated here, to avoid drift between two source-of-truth files.

proposed_integration_patches.action: OPTIONAL: append a one-sentence preemption to the proposed Part 6.13 prose, addressing the c4 advocate_mode 'dome silent on extra-firmamental geometry' defense (defense_survives=3). Recommended insertion point: end of the final paragraph (before the closing </details>). This is an optional pre-integration tightening; if the decider prefers to integrate as-is and let a follow-on EXP add the preemption after curmudgeon's adversarial pass, that is also acceptable.
proposed_integration_patches.proposed_addendum_text: A defender might respond that the dome model is silent on extra-firmamental geometry, not committed against it — that the aether could extend outward with as-yet-unspecified topology and so Mpc-scale distances might exist in dome coordinates we have not yet derived. This move fails because the WINs grouped here cite Cepheid, SBF, and TRGB distance values, RAR baryonic accelerations, El Gordo cluster masses at z=0.87, and CMB low-multipole alignments as confirmations of the dome. The dome's adoption of those measurements as confirmation is its explicit commitment to literal Mpc-scale distances and to a CMB observable at the dome's location. A dome that endorses Mpc-scale Cepheid distances at the wins.html level cannot then deny those distances at the geometry level without internal contradiction — and a copper firmament that endorses CMB observations as confirmation cannot also block those same observations as opaque. The contradiction is between the WIN page and the model's published geometry, both of which the dome owns.
proposed_integration_patches.adapted_from: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-055.c4.json:advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation (extended to also cover the WIN-048 copper-firmament/CMB self-contradiction explicitly)

proposed_integration_patches.action: After integration, verify: (a) grep 'p6-613-mond-grouping' in data/sections.json returns exactly 1 hit; (b) grep '"6.13' in data/sections.json returns exactly 1 hit (only the subsection title); (c) grep '8,619' returns multiple hits but each is in the bracketed 'rim' phrasing (apex/rim wording fix verified); (d) grep 'kill-shots tab where anti-globe ammunition' returns 0 hits (legacy operator draft phrasing must not slip through); (e) the numbering 6.12 → 6.13 → 6.14 reads cleanly in the rendered part6 HTML.

counter_arguments_considered.other_side_per_user_pref: I gave Option (ii) — close ISS-2103 as already-resolved, do NOT integrate Part 6.13 — a real airing in recommendation_chain[1]. The strongest version of that argument is: 9 days of non-integration with no operator nudge could legitimately be read as implicit deprioritization, and the dangling-pointer problem ISS-2103 nominally tracks has already been resolved by ISS-2025. A reasonable reviewer could close this issue as moot, with the implicit Part 6.13 audit trail living forever in EXP-295 and the live site staying lean.
counter_arguments_considered.why_option_i_still_wins: The pipeline's operating principle is that operator notes are standing instructions until explicitly retracted, not deprecated by silence. EXP-295 Decision 2 was explicit ('Option D — reclassify AND add a Part 6 subsection'), and the curmudgeon consolidation review (2026-05-09T18:40Z) endorsed that choice. Treating silence as veto would invert the default in a way that would, applied generally, slowly erode every multi-patch EXP that had any single patch slip through. Option (i) restores the EXP-295 package to its operator-specified shape; option (ii) accepts a quiet failure mode and freezes it.
counter_arguments_considered.weakest_assumption_in_my_recommendation: I'm assuming the 9-day non-integration was a decider scheduling/dispatch miss rather than an intentional choice. If the operator deliberately did NOT integrate Part 6.13 (e.g., decided post-EXP-295 that the verdict reclassifications were sufficient and the section was unnecessary), then a one-line HNOTE retracting Option D would close ISS-2103 cleanly under (ii) and supersede this expansion. Recommend the decider check for any such operator note before pushing to queue.

attention_inbox_signal_for_tinker.summary: WIN-055.c4 hole #2 and hole #3 both inherit text from c3 that became stale after ISS-2025 closed (2026-05-16T17:24Z). c4 (2026-05-17T22:55Z) ran 29 hours later but did not re-verify the 'See Part 6.13' / 'cosmological-cluster section' wording it inherited from c3. The downstream effect: decider routed an issue (ISS-2103) whose premise was 2 days stale. Worth tinker investigating whether c-cycle reviews systematically re-verify carry-over hole text against current data/wins.json or just inherit it.

suggested_inbox_payload.reason: Pipeline hygiene: c-cycle carry-over re-verification gap. WIN-055.c4 inherited stale c3 hole text about 'See Part 6.13' references that had been removed by ISS-2025 2 days earlier. Symptom: ISS-2103 generated this run with a partially stale premise. Likely class: 'minor process improvement, not a blocker'.

tldr_evidence: ISS-2103 says five WINs reference a non-existent Part 6.13. Verification against current data/wins.json shows the dangling cross-references were already removed by ISS-2025 (closed 2026-05-16). But the underlying decision — operator's Option D in EXP-295, to integrate a Part 6.13 cross-cutting subsection alongside the five verdict reclassifications — was never executed. The shovel-ready prose (with the 8,619 km wording fix already applied) sits in EXP-295 awaiting the decider to push it to the integration queue.
tldr_verdict: Recommend integrate Part 6.13 from EXP-295 P0 (operator's standing Option D choice, curmudgeon-endorsed, anchor still clean, gap between Part 6.12 and 6.14 is a single newline). Alternative — close ISS-2103 as superseded-and-deprioritized — is plausible but treats operator silence as implicit veto, which inverts the pipeline's default. Optional: append one preemption sentence (from c4 advocate_mode) to address the 'dome silent on extra-firmamental geometry' defense before publish.
notes_for_decider: This EXP-424 is a routing/decision document, NOT a re-write of EXP-295. Do not duplicate the 13.8 KB of Part 6.13 prose anywhere; route EXP-295 directly.

Recommended priority queue parameters: queue_id (decider-assigned), source='analyst-EXP-424', class='deep-attack', target_type='section-new', review_class='deep-attack' (per c4 advocate-mode defense_survives=3).

If pushing to the queue: please also close (or supersede) ISS-2103 in the same decider run with a note like 'Routed to integration via EXP-295 P0; sub_rationale dangling-pointer half of the issue was already resolved by ISS-2025 2026-05-16'.

Before pushing: spot-check operator's human-notes.json for any retraction of Option D. Last operator HNOTE relevant to this scope was HNOTE-OPERATOR-EXP-295-MOND-8619-WORDING-FIX-001 (2026-05-10) which assumed Part 6.13 would integrate.
After integration: the c4 hole #2 (sub_rationale not rendered on live site) remains separately tracked under ISS-2102 — that's outside ISS-2103's scope and outside this EXP.
